Excel files can have merged cells. The value needs to be copied to all cells that are merged otherwise what is being read in the stream is not correct. This also affects header parsing since people use merged cells in headers as well.
eg.
Set of merged cells 

| | | | |

| | | | |
| | | | |

|a|a|a|a|
|a|a|a|a|

issue_title: Tiebreaker in _TypeResolver
issue_body:
_TypeResolver should default to calling something a string if there is a tie between string and another type.

Currently I am experiencing inconsistent behaviour for calls like 

    import unittest
    from io import BytesIO
    
    from tabulator import Stream
    from tableschema import infer
    
    class BooleanTestCase(unittest.TestCase):
        def test_check_type(self):
    
            data = BytesIO(b"foo\tbaraz\t\n1\ttst\tm tst\n\tf\tother test")
    
            with Stream(data, format="tsv") as stream:
                sample = stream.sample
            schema = infer(sample[0], sample[1:])
            assert schema["fields"][1]["type"] == "string"

Here there is a tie between boolean and string. If the test is run on its own then 1/2 the time it fails. I think that the best way to follow "in the face of ambiguity refuse the temptation to guess" would be for the typeresolver to just stick to string.

issue_title: Switch to go 1.5 for product build / test
issue_body: Improvements in the new Go compiler toolchain suggest significant performance benefits. However, Deis' integration tests use stdout naively and will break if `GOMAXPROCS > 1`. So for the time being we could upgrade CI nodes to Go 1.5 but add `GOMAXPROCS=1` to the CI test jobs.

issue_title: `globus task wait` doesn't respect `--format JSON`
issue_body:
Task wait accepts the `--format` option, but it doesn't actually produce JSON output.

I'm not sure that it needs to support `--heartbeat --format JSON` because streaming JSON output is messy. We could print heartbeats to stderr.

When done, there should at least be a result document with the final task status.

issue_title: Move configuration for non production Globus environments into the SDK?
issue_body:
In a recent discussions about our deployment processes, we raised the possibility of testing the SDK against non-production Globus resources (i.e. internal prerelease services).

On a related note, the SDK defines tooling to make it possible to dispatch to non production services, as it must, in an environment variable.

The question that I'd raise is this: should we stop dancing around this issue and make the requisite configuration part of the SDK itself? It makes our lives easier, but publicly exposes some non-public assets.

My gut call is that the trade-off is worth it.

issue_title: fish shell requires adding ~/miniconda3/bin/ to $PATH in conda 4.4.0rc1 (unlike bash-shell-derivatives)
issue_body:
Edit:

Adding the following lines to my config.fish file fixed the problem on 4.3.25, and per @jaimergp this should also work on 4.4.0rc1:

```
# Conda startup script
source ~/miniconda3/etc/fish/conf.d/conda.fish

# Path to system Python3
set fish_user_paths $fish_user_paths /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/bin/

# For Conda < 4.4.0 prepend Conda path to fish path (should be removable in Conda 4.4.0+)
set fish_user_paths $fish_user_paths ~/miniconda3/bin/
```
**However**, fish shell users don't yet have a shell script which allows for the removal of ~/miniconda3/bin/ from the system path (unlike bash shell).

### Edited the subject line to clarify the issue.
-------
Original issue text:


I've done a lot of digging into this and can't seem to figure it out. I see that conda previously had fish support (e.g. https://github.com/conda/conda/pull/545, #2611, #2680, #5182, etc.) but I'm not having any luck getting it to work on 4.40rc1 (Python 3.6 version for OS X).

`> source ~/miniconda3/etc/fish/conf.d/conda.fish` completes silently, without error.

Subsequently, running 'conda' from a fish shell results in the following:

```
> conda
fish: Unknown command 'conda'
conda.fish (line 2): 
        command conda
                ^
in function 'conda'
	called on standard input
```

I'm running macOS Sierra (10.12.6).

/cc some fish conda users to see whether any of you have tried 4.4.0 and are experiencing similar issues:
@jaimergp @zzag @soxofaan @rsully @alexbw @abhigenie92 @msarahan @kalefranz

issue_title: Improve conda clean --packages
issue_body:
I do understand that there is a [TODO comment in the source code](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/ebc6a353d6d141f0b2e15e03b0d4cad469f3c957/conda/cli/main_clean.py#L260-L261), but `conda clean --packages` assumes that all conda environments are either hard-linked or completely copied, and completely ignores the fact that packages can be also symlinked.

I hit the issue that old python packages are not get cleaned due to the hard links inside the packages (just like the TODO comment mentioned above warned us), and reading the code and searching the internet I came across only [one discussion on Continuum Google Groups](https://groups.google.com/a/continuum.io/forum/#!topic/conda/NANWcX64dIA), so I figured that it might be worthwhile to create an issue for this.

issue_title: Minimizing package changes in the solver
issue_body: Recently ran into a problem over at Dask, which I expect wouldn't be an issue if the solver tried to minimize the number of packages that are swapped out (e.g. upgraded, downgraded, etc.). Please see issue ( https://github.com/dask/dask/issues/2191 ) for an example of this kind of problem. I expect this is generally helpful and useful to others. Though would be curious to hear some thoughts and feedback on this suggestion.

issue_title: Error in PackageNotFoundError

issue_title: Comment regex fails with strings containing a # character
issue_body:
config.ini
```
[foo]
bar = ['lala', 'lele', 'here comes the # but this is not a comment']
```

Example:
`mat = self._nolistvalue.match(value)`
results in the following matching groups:
```
0: ['lala', 'lele', 'here comes the 
1:  but this is not a comment']
```

Replacing
`(?:[^'"\#].*?)|     # unquoted`
with
`(?:[^'"\#].*)|     # unquoted`
resolves the problem BUT leads to not correctly regexing real comments.

No idea how to easily fix that without rewriting the whole regex.

issue_title: PID coeff in conversion for xl-320
issue_body:
Maybe there is an explanation, but for me the coeff. for the PID gain is a little bit weird : 

```
def dxl_to_pid(value, model):
    return (value[0] * 0.004,
            value[1] * 0.48828125,
            value[2] * 0.125)


def pid_to_dxl(value, model):
    truncate = lambda x: int(max(0, min(x, 254)))
    return [truncate(x * y) for x, y in zip(value, (250, 2.048, 8.0))]
```

Because PID are all beetween 0 and 254 means that when you set in pypot range of : 
P (value[2])  is between 0 and  31,75
I (value[1]) is between 0 and 124,0234375
D (value[0]) is between 0 and 1,016

A good PID in pypot could be (8.0, 15.625, 0.064), I find this not really "readable"

issue_title: [Advisor] Issues to resolve prior to Advisor module GA
issue_body:
- [ ] `advisor configuration get` should be split into `advisor configuration list` since it returns a collection. `--resource-group` should not be an optional parameter since, as far as I can tell, a resource group can only have a single configuration.
- [ ] There should be an `advisor configuration show` command that optionally accepts resource group and shows the single configuration for that group (or if omitted, the subscription config).
- [ ]  `advisor configuration get` returns a format with `nextLink` and `value`.  It should only return the contents of value. Any paging should be automatically handled by autorest.
- [ ] Since `exclude` is a property of a an Advisor configuration object, `--exclude` should be a three-state flag and `--include` is not needed. 
- [ ] The commands lack examples.  When I try to create a configuration for a resource group I just get an object with `code: ImproperScope`.
- [ ] `advisor configuration set` should be called `advisor configuration update` as it displays PATCH-like behavior, not PUT-like behavior.
- [ ] recommendation: tests should use the built-in checks syntax instead of getting outputs in json and making assertions on them.
- [ ] For me, `az advisor recommendation generate` produces no output. If there are no recommendations, something to that effect should be output.
- [ ] `az advisor configuration set` should return the updated object instead of forcing the user to make a subsequent call to get.
- [ ] The relationship between `recommendation generate` and `recommendation list` is not clear to me. Generate appears to do nothing and list returns an empty collection. Speculating here based on how the `configuration set` command works, I suspect that generate should immediately follow up with a list when it completes.
- [ ] It seems like there is configuration stuff happening in `advisor configuration` and `advisor recommendation` (enable/disable).  Not sure why this is.
- [ ] The `recommendation enable/disable` commands could logically be folded into a single command.
- [ ] The help text for `--ids` in the recommendation enable/disable commands makes reference to "Resource Id" arguments. However, these aren't in a Resource Id Arguments group. You should be able to specify the resource by names (resource group and configuration name).
- [ ] If there can only be a single configuration object per resource group, you could also simply make the enable/disable commands accept the resource group name (if you can enable/disable the subscription level one, there would need to be a mechanism for that).

---

### Environment summary

Install Method (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, edge build) / CLI version (`az --version`) / OS version / Shell Type (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  

```
azure-cli 2.0.25
```

issue_title: The test log generated by nosetest is empty
`--ids` parameter only supports 3 levels
issue_body:
Azure Backup has IDs that look like this:

`/Subscriptions/00000/resourceGroups/RG/providers/Microsoft.RecoveryServices/vaults/{vault_name}/backupFabrics/{fabric_name}/protectionContainers/{container_name}/protectedItems/{item_name}/recoveryPoint/{recovery_point_guid}`

Thus they have a 5 level child resource hierarchy.  Our current `--ids` infrastructure only supports "resource", "child" and "grandchild" levels.

We should revise this feature to support an arbitrary number of things. For example, instead of:

```
        - child_namespace       Namespace for the child resoure (optional)
        - child_type            Type of the child resource
        - child_name            Name of the child resource
        - grandchild_namespace  Namespace for the grandchild resource (optional)
        - grandchild_type       Type of the grandchild resource
        - grandchild_name       Name of the grandchild resource
```

We should allow:
```
        - child1_namespace
        - child1_type            
        - child1_name          
        - child2_namespace 
        - child2_type       
        - child2_name       
```
And so forth.  This would impact the way you would need to annotate "id_part" as well as changing the structure of the dictionary "parse_resource_id" returns. 

cc/ @dragonfly91

issue_title: az network nic create gives confusing/incorrect error message
issue_body:
Repro: 

Create new (empty) resource group: 
  az resource group create -l westus -n reprotestgroup

Add a nic to the group:
  az network nic create -g reprotestgroup -n testnic --subnet somesubnet

Result:
Error ... Referenced resource /subscriptions/.../resourceGroups/reprotestgroup/..../virtualNetworks/_None_/.../subnets/somesubnet does not exist...

First comment; it is not clear that you have to specify the name of an existing virtual network if you specify a name and not an id for subnet. Neither the usage string nor the help for --subnet tells you this. You have to look at the help for the optional --vnet-name parameter to see under what circumstances it is required. At a minimum, this description should be on --subnet parameter since that is the parameter that introduced the constraint.

Second comment: The usage string should make clear the dependency between the parameters.

Third comment: There is no validation for missing vnet names in the case of providing an name for --subnet. Instead it uses "None" as the name. Very unlikely that this is what the user intended.

issue_title: [Monitor] aler-rules create should take "actions" as JSON encoded parameters like conditions
issue_body:
### Description
`az monitor alert-rules create` consumes JSON encoded `conditions` but not `actions`. 

`actions` should be modeled the same way as `conditions`


Reported by @davidmu1

issue_title: az storage entity query: error: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
With azure-cli 2.0.32, az storage entity query no longer works correctly.

**To Reproduce**
From bash run (where capitalized strings are the appropriate values):
docker run -it microsoft/azure-cli
az storage entity query --account-name ACCOUNT_NAME --account-key ACCOUNT_KEY --table-name TABLE_NAME

The azure cli will error saying "az storage entity query: error: 'NoneType' object is not iterable" (regardless of whether the specified table exists)

**Expected behavior**
If the table exists, this should result in a description of the contents of the table being returned. If not, it should result in an error saying that the table does not exist.

**Environment summary**
Repros on Ubuntu/Debian with version 2.0.32 of azure-cli.

**Additional context**
This command works as expected with azure-cli 2.0.31. Version 2.0.32 appears to have introduced a regression.

issue_title: Exclude untracked files (pytest + coverage)
issue_body:
I'm currently trying out running pytest together with a coverage target as a pre-commit hook. This is problematic when there are untracked files present, because a commit should not be prevented by test failures or missing test coverage of untracked files (which are not getting committed, after all).

For this to work, and unless I'm missing something, it currently seems necessary to manually stash untracked files (exclusively) before `pre-commit run`, which comes down to doing something like: 

```
$ git stash --keep-index && \
git stash --keep-index --include-untracked && \
git stash pop stash@{1}
$ pre-commit run
$ git clean -f && git stash pop
```

Could pre-commit be made to do this for me and stash and restore untracked files, just like it's already hiding unstaged changes? This seems to me the cleanest way to have all hooks run in an environment that looks like the actual commit.

For what it's worth, I haven't found a good way to configure a `system` hook to work around unstaged files. As tests are not necessarily in the same file as the tested code, I think `pytest` cannot just run for files with staged changes: the test might be in an unchanged file. Also, the coverage plugin for pytest offers no command-line arguments for changing the files coverage looks at; therefore, `coverage` needs to be invoked directly. If I'm not mistaken, the resulting hook would somehow need to run this command (for an arbitrary coverage target of 99%):

```
$ coverage run -m pytest --ignore=$UNSTAGED_FILES && \
coverage report --omit=$UNSTAGED_FILES --fail-under=99
```

I could probably put this in a dedicated script. However, it would be much nicer if pre-commit took care of unstaged files for me.

*edit* In the above command, `$UNSTAGED_FILES` should be `$UNTRACKED_FILES`, of course. Sorry for the confusion.

issue_title: `stages: [commit]` hooks will run with `pre-commit run otherhookid`
issue_body:
minor logic bug, good new-contributor ticket

Easy to reproduce on pre-commit itself:

```diff
diff --git a/.pre-commit-config.yaml b/.pre-commit-config.yaml
index a146bd2..7bb382d 100644
--- a/.pre-commit-config.yaml
+++ b/.pre-commit-config.yaml
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@ repos:
     rev: v1.2.3
     hooks:
     -   id: trailing-whitespace
+        stages: [commit]
     -   id: end-of-file-fixer
     -   id: autopep8-wrapper
     -   id: check-docstring-first
```

```console
$ pre-commit run end-of-file-fixer --all-files
Trim Trailing Whitespace.................................................Passed
Fix End of Files.........................................................Passed
```

issue_title: Centralize Notebook "Development Installation" docs?
issue_body:
There was already some related activity (#590, #1039, #1134, #754), but I personally still had a hard time to find the right information about how to make a development installation of the Jupyter notebook.

I found (partial) documentation in three different places, with partly divergent instructions:

https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md
https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/README.md#development-installation-quickstart
https://jupyter-notebook.readthedocs.org/en/latest/development_js.html

The last link (which I also found last), actually helped me to successfully create a development installation on my computer, the others did more harm than good.

I'd suggest to collect all the information in a single place (e.g. `CONTRIBUTING.rst`).
This could then be linked to in the `README.md` and it could be included (with the `.. include::` directive) into the online docs (replacing what's there now).

Would this be a good idea?
Or am I way off?

If there is some interest, I'd be willing to make a PR with my suggested changes, but I'd need some help determining which of the instructions are actually still accurate and which aren't.

issue_title: get_range_endpoints: support of float values
issue_body:
`get_range_endpoints()` uses `random.randint()` to produce random numbers while creating mock objects.

The issue is that `FloatType` fails to create mock objects, if it has float values for `min_value` and `max_value` properties.

E.g., having field

``` python

delay = FloatType(
    min_value=1.0,
    max_value=30.0,
    default=10.0,
    required=True,
)
```

will raise an error

```ServerConfig.get_mock_object().to_primitive()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
    ServerConfig.get_mock_object().to_primitive()
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/models.py", line 378, in get_mock_object
    values[name] = field.mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 323, in mock
    return self._mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/compound.py", line 115, in _mock
    return self.model_class.get_mock_object(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/models.py", line 378, in get_mock_object
    values[name] = field.mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 323, in mock
    return self._mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/compound.py", line 115, in _mock
    return self.model_class.get_mock_object(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/models.py", line 378, in get_mock_object
    values[name] = field.mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 323, in mock
    return self._mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/compound.py", line 115, in _mock
    return self.model_class.get_mock_object(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/models.py", line 378, in get_mock_object
    values[name] = field.mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 323, in mock
    return self._mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 450, in _mock
    return get_value_in(self.min_value, self.max_value)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 71, in get_value_in
    *get_range_endpoints(min_length, max_length, padding, required_length))
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/random.py", line 218, in randint
    return self.randrange(a, b+1)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/random.py", line 182, in randrange
    raise ValueError("non-integer arg 1 for randrange()")
ValueError: non-integer arg 1 for randrange()
```

Obviously, `random.randint` cannot be used for creating random floats. `random.uniform` must do this job.

`get_value_in` is also used by `StringType` and so on. The most simple approach is to select random generator basing on type of `min_length` and `max_length` parameters, e.g.:

``` python

def get_value_in(min_length, max_length, padding=0, required_length=0):
    generator = (
        random.uniform
        if isinstance(min_length, float) or isinstance(max_length, float) else
        random.randint
    )
    return generator(
        *get_range_endpoints(min_length, max_length, padding, required_length))
```

But, IMHO, this is a bit dirty way. It's better to specify proper generator in type/field definition. This will also allow user to set own generator, e.g. from `faker` library.

Thanks

issue_title: Decide on versioning policy for API endpoints
issue_body:
The Blockstack Core API currently uses a "coarse" versioning system where version 1 is placed in the sub-directory `/v1`. The thinking is that once the API is stable, we will commit to supporting `/v1/`.

One of the biggest frustrations as the user of an API is refactoring your code for changes to the API. Along the same lines, one of the biggest frustrations for the developer of the API is being unable to change it for fear of breaking backward compatibility.

There's two options here:

1. Continue with coarse versioning. The problem here is that as developers come to rely on not just the endpoint existing, but returning a specific JSON schema, slight changes in the API will need to bump the version number.

2. Move to a more finely grained approach. Allow apps to request the version of the API for which they were designed and use a compatibility layer to translate.

My feeling is that option (1) will devolve into option (2) over time, leaving behind a lot of ad hoc translation code. If we commit to option (2), we may end up with an easier codebase to manage over time.

issue_title: Make the old resolver compatible with the new API
issue_body: We have an old resolver interface that ran as a separate system. Now we're merging it with the new API. It'd be great if the changes to that interface are minimal and other parties like OpenBazaar and the Onename API don't need to push changes at their end when we upgrade our resolver.

issue_title: make django a requirement, or somehow let users know that it has to be installed
issue_body:
Hi,

thanks for an awesome plugin; it was a relief to find when I introduced pylint to an existing django project and was swarmed with errors from django `class Meta` and so forth.

My team had an issue with getting the plugin to work in some instances.

Specifically; using a virtualenv/global installation of pylint and plylint django which did not include django. It may seems silly, but I think it's a valid usage pattern.

The django typical lint errors kept being reported, and we were pretty confused as to why. Of course, django had to be installed on path.

I didn't see anywhere in the readme that django had to be installed. It's a minor nuisance to solve, but it'd be nice to help the next person by either:

* letting them know in the readme
* raising an error when pylint_django is loaded if django is not installed
* adding django to requirements in `setup.py`

I'm in favour of the last option. Glad to create a PR for the option that the maintainer(s) prefer, if any.

Thanks

issue_title: Lack of colorama hard dependency
issue_body:
**Description of issue or feature request**:
The current release blows up because `colorama` is not a dependency. Although it *should* get pulled in by either `cryptography` or `pynacl`, there is no information to describe that is the issue when using securesystemslib.

For example, this is the test log from `in-toto` using the latest securesystemslib (given that it doesn't hard depend on either library)

```
======================================================================
ERROR: tests.test_runlib (unittest.loader._FailedTest)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
ImportError: Failed to import test module: tests.test_runlib
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.6/unittest/loader.py", line 428, in _find_test_path
    module = self._get_module_from_name(name)
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.6/unittest/loader.py", line 369, in _get_module_from_name
    __import__(name)
  File "/home/santiago/Documents/personal/programas/in-toto/in-toto/tests/test_runlib.py", line 29, in <module>
    from in_toto.models.metadata import Metablock
  File "/home/santiago/Documents/personal/programas/in-toto/in-toto/in_toto/models/metadata.py", line 34, in <module>
    from in_toto.models.layout import Layout
  File "/home/santiago/Documents/personal/programas/in-toto/in-toto/in_toto/models/layout.py", line 49, in <module>
    import securesystemslib.interface
  File "/home/santiago/Documents/personal/programas/in-toto/in-toto/.tox/py36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/securesystemslib/interface.py", line 51, in <module>
    from colorama import Fore
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'colorama'
```

Needless to say, it's rather dangerous to assume that `cryptography` or `pynacl` will depend on colorama forever. Worse than that, I'm assuming that we may add support for other libraries which may or may not pull `colorama` in.

**Current behavior**:
Thing breaks unless you explicitly:

1. Install a dependency that pulls `colorama`, such as `pynacl` or `cryptography`.
2. Install colorama explicitly to compensate for this lack of hard-dependency

**Expected behavior**:
The library installs itself with everything it needs and doesn't make any assumptions of the packages that will be pulled and their upstream dependencies.

issue_title: Issue when parsing ISO8601 date string on GAE
issue_body:
I'm using version 2.4.1 in both a local GAE environment as well as in a local ipython instance. 
In both I attempt to parse the string using:
```
asdf = dateutil.parser.parse(u'2014-06-01T00:00:00.000Z')
print asdf.tzinfo
```
On the local machine, this properly shows tzutc as the result
When using the GAE environment, this instead returns a datetime with tzlocal

It looks like this is being set in parser.py at line 424 (425 after my pdb insertion)
```

422  	                ret = ret.replace(tzinfo=tzinfo)

```

I'm not sure what the issue is but this seems very strange.

issue_title: Raspberry - STT, Assistant, TTS response time
issue_body:
Hi, 

I made an assistant that uses Watson's STT, Assistant and TTS. 

In my code I used pyaudio (like in the example provided in microphone-speech-to-text.py) to record 2-3 seconds of audio, then I pass that audio to Watson STT using speech_to_text.recognize which returns a text that I pass to Watson Assistant. Then I pass the result to Waton TTS (always using the code provided in the example) which then give me a .wav that I play using vlc.MediaPlayer. 

I did some testing on raspberry and the problem is that the whole response time is too long. I have different intents on my assistant and I tested them all putting on an excel file the response time of each service. These are the average responses time: STT 3,48 seconds, Assistant 0,62 seconds, TTS 2 seconds. On average the response in audio is played after 6 seconds which is a very long time. 
My question is: Is it somehow possible to reduce the response time? STT is the service that takes too much to return a result and in some cases it takes 5 seconds. Here is the excel file of response time
[tempi.xlsx](https://github.com/watson-developer-cloud/python-sdk/files/2202202/tempi.xlsx)



STT
```
transcript = ''
	#mettere il model
    try:
        with open(recording_name,'rb') as f:
            dictResponse = speech_to_text.recognize(audio=f, content_type='audio/l16; rate=44100')
            #print(json.dumps(dictResponse,indent=2))
            if dictResponse["results"]:
                transcript = dictResponse["results"][0]["alternatives"][0]["transcript"]
                print('transcript: {}'.format(transcript))
            else:
                print("no results from stt")
                transcript = ""
    except Exception as e:
        print('speech2text error: {}'.format(e))

    doneSTT = time.time()
    print("--> STT time: {}".format(doneSTT-doneRecording))
```

Assistant
```
#input to watson assistant
    response=''
    try:
        response = assistant.message(
        workspace_id=workspace_id,
        input={
            'text': transcript
        },
        context={
            'metadata': {
                'deployment': 'myDeployment'
            }
        })
    except Exception as e:
        print("Assistant error: {}".format(e))
    
    
        
    
    #watson response
    #print(json.dumps(response,indent=2))
    if response["intents"]:
        intents = response["intents"][0]
        intent = intents["intent"]
        intentConfidence = response["intents"][0]["confidence"]
        print('intent name: {}, confidence: {}'.format(intent, intentConfidence))
    else:
        print("intent not found")


        if response["output"]:
            risposta = response["output"]["text"][0]
            print('risposta: {}'.format(risposta))
        else:
            print("no output from assistant")
```

TTS and response playback
```
try:
            with open(outputName,'wb') as audio_file:
                audio_file.write(text_to_speech.synthesize(risposta, accept='audio/wav',voice="en-US_AllisonVoice").content)
            #audio_file.write(text_to_speech.synthesize(risposta, accept='audio/wav',voice="it-IT_FrancescaVoice").content)
        except Exception as e:

        doneTTS = time.time()

```

Any help would be appreciated, thanks.

1.5

default python3 on raspberry

issue_title: Instantiating ToneAnalyzerV3Beta should have default version value
issue_body:
The example code suggests you can instantiate a tone analyzer by passing in just 2 arguments, as follows: 

`ta = ToneAnalyzerV3Beta(username='yourusername',password='yourpassword')`

However, this is not right -- you have to pass in a version argument as well, thusly:

`tone_analyzer = ToneAnalyzerV3Beta(version='2016-02-11', username='yourusername', password='yourpassword')`

Since it doesnt seem obvious what the right version argument should be, maybe this should default to ToneAnalyzerV3Beta.latest_version?

issue_title: Apply @validates decorator multiple times?
post_dump pass a list of objects as original object
issue_body:
Hi,

I think post_dump with pass_original=True should pass the original object related to the data serialized and not a list of objects which this object belongs to.

```python
from marshmallow import fields, post_dump, Schema

class DeviceSchema(Schema):
    id = fields.String()

    @post_dump(pass_original=True)
    def __post_dump(self, data, obj):
        print(obj)  # <-- this is a list

devices = [dict(id=1), dict(id=2)]
DeviceSchema().dump(devices, many=True)
```
In the above example, the parameter `obj` is a list of devices rather than the device object itself.

What do you think?

issue_title: required_for_dump and required_for_load options
issue_body:
I have a schema where a field may optionally be provided to `load` but must by provided by `dump`. I currently support this with code like this:

```
class OptionalLoad(Schema):
    another_field = fields.String()
    optional_load_required_dump = fields.DateTime()

class RequiredDump(OptionalLoad):
    optional_load_required_dump = fields.DateTime(required=True)
```

It would be convenient if there were a pair of new arguments that allowed something like:

```
class Better(Schema):
    optional_load_required_dump = fields.DateTime(required_for_dump=True)
    optional_dump_required_load = fields.DateTime(required_for_load=True)
```

Happy to submit a patch if this is desirable.

issue_title: How to validate unique together constraint?
issue_body:
Excuse me for asking this question here, but I could not find solution and also got no answer on the mailing list.

I'm using Tastypie 0.9.16 and Django 1.3.1 for compatibility requirement in the project.

Restrito model has a unique_together = ('usuario', 'titulo'), and I have two resources, UserResource and RestritoResource:

```python
class UserResource(ModelResource):
    restritos = fields.ToManyField('geral.api.resources.RestritoResource', 'restrito_set', related_name='usuario', null=True)

    class Meta:
        queryset = User.objects.all()
        fields = ['username', 'email', 'first_name', 'last_name']
        authentication = Authentication()
        authorization = Authorization()
        always_return_data = True
        validation = FormValidation(form_class=UserValidationForm)
        filtering = {
            'username': ALL,
        }

class RestritoResource(ModelResource):
    usuario = fields.ForeignKey(UserResource, 'usuario', full=True)
    titulo = fields.ForeignKey(TituloResource, attribute='titulo')

    class Meta:
        allowed_methods = ['get', 'post', 'put', 'delete', 'patch']
        authentication = Authentication()
        authorization = Authorization()
        queryset = Restrito.objects.all()
        resource_name = 'restrito'
        always_return_data = True
        fields = ['usuario', 'titulo', 'ativa']
        serializer = Serializer()
        validation = FormValidation(form_class=RestritoValidationForm)
        filtering = {
            'usuario': ALL_WITH_RELATIONS,
        }
```
I need to validate a unique constraint and imagine a ModelForm can handle it, but when I submit a post like this.
```json
{
  "titulo": "/geral/api/v1/titulo/688/",
  "usuario": {
    "email": "johnny@mail.com",
    "nome": "johnny"
  }
}
```
I get this error:

"error_message": "int () argument must be a string or a number, not 'dict'"

If i remove validation from Meta everything works as expected.

It has some detail to work with FormValidation I'm leaving aside? Or I Unable to validate nested resources with Form?

Thanks

issue_title: Senza traffic should wait for stack to be ready to change traffic
issue_body:
### Current Behaviour

When running change traffic `senza traffic` on stack recently deployed. The status code of the stack is not ready (`(CRETE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE`) and Senza fails trying to update it.
### Cause of the Problem

It is not possible to update CF stack when it's status code is not in `(CRETE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE` (or we should not change when it is not in one of those status code).
### Expected Behaviour

Senza should wait the stack to get in a final status code if the current status of the stack is `(CREATE|UPDATE)_IN_PROGRESS` and ending up in some good state `(CREATE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE`.

issue_title: Poor performance dealing with regular expression which is always satisfiable
issue_body:
Is there any way to make the regular expression problem below perform better. Z3 has trouble deciding that the regular expression (Which is essentially .*) accepts all characters.

```
(declare-fun X () String)  
(assert (not (str.in.re X (re.* (re.range "\x00" "\xFF")))))
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```

issue_title: Does Z3_solver returned by ``Z3_mk_simple_solver()`` and ``Z3_mk_solver_from_tactic()`` require reference counting?
issue_body:
In the C API documentation

-  ``Z3_mk_solver()``
-  ``Z3_mk_solver_for_logic()``

have both have the comment

```
/*

...
       \remark User must use #Z3_solver_inc_ref and #Z3_solver_dec_ref to manage solver objects.
       Even if the context was created using #Z3_mk_context instead of #Z3_mk_context_rc.
...
*/
```

however ``Z3_mk_simple_solver()`` and ``Z3_mk_solver_from_tactic()`` do not have this comment. Is this just an omission or do ``Z3_solver`` objects returned from these two calls **not need reference counting calls**?

issue_title: Support later versions of STIX 2
issue_body: We need to figure out how to support later versions of STIX 2 in this library. It should probably default to the latest published version of the spec, with an option to set the version to validate against. People may want to validate against multiple versions of the spec if they are supporting multiple versions at once. Each version of the validator could have a maximum supported STIX version, or we could do something with subpackages like https://github.com/oasis-open/cti-python-stix2/issues/79. I'm leaning towards the latter.

issue_title: Add RReLu nonlinearity
issue_body:
Some of you may have seen it, but the paper [Empirical Evaluation of Rectified Activations in Convolutional Network](http://arxiv.org/abs/1505.00853) compares several rectifier variants, and shows that Randomized ReLUs (uniform random a during training) can outperform PReLUs on CIFAR-10 & CIFAR-100 on the test set. RReLus have been [discussued](https://github.com/torch/nn/issues/380), [merged](https://github.com/torch/nn/pull/437), and [documented](https://github.com/torch/nn/blob/master/doc/transfer.md#rrelu) in Torch. That documentation link above also gives a fairly straightforward expression of what RReLus are.

I suspect it shouldn't be too hard to add support for RReLus in Lasagne, but I have little experience with the Lasagne internals. I believe it should simply be a matter of copying [`LeakyRectify`](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/nonlinearities.py#L160), replacing the leakiness with min+max, setting up a [Theano shared random stream](http://deeplearning.net/software/theano/tutorial/examples.html#brief-example), and using that to pick the leakiness. I'm not entirely sure how to make the leakiness be `(l+u)/2` during evaluation though...

issue_title: Add PReLU activation
issue_body:
I'm thinking of implementing this within the framework, but not totally sure how to go about it in a holistic manner. 

Right now the assumptions in the code are that layer.params involve W and b, as per usual, and these are explicitly grabbed by all the necessary functions, one by one. 

The only naive way I can really think of doing it is adding another nasty if statement inside each layer type to grab the additional parameters if they exist, which isn't very scalable. 

Perhaps we should encapsulate layer params in something more modular with an interface that deals with the logic elsewhere? Would this be something people are interested in?

Ideas/thoughts before I take the time to really think through that path?

issue_title: Missed loop by LoopFinder
issue_body:
Hi, 
As discussed with @rhelmot on slack, our new `LoopFinder` (after the refactor) misses loops that contains function calls, e.g. `while (foo) { bar(); }`; while removing the call to `bar()` makes the loop to be detected again.

Here's an example:
ARM assembly code **with** function call inside loop:
```
  10426:   2364        movs    r3, #100    ; 0x64
  10428:   603b        str r3, [r7, #0]
  1042a:   e003        b.n 10434 <main+0x14>
  1042c:   2101        movs    r1, #1                       -> loop body node
  1042e:   6838        ldr r0, [r7, #0]
  10430:   f7ff ffe9   bl  10406 <foo>                     -> function call inside of loop
  10434:   687b        ldr r3, [r7, #4]                       -> loop entry
  10436:   1c5a        adds    r2, r3, #1
  10438:   607a        str r2, [r7, #4]
  1043a:   2b09        cmp r3, #9
  1043c:   d9f6        bls.n   1042c <main+0xc>
  1043e:   2300        movs    r3, #0
  10440:   4618        mov r0, r3
   ....
```
However, the loop is not detected: `len(loop_finder.loops) = 0`.

ARM assembly code **without** function call inside loop:
```
 275    10426:   2364        movs    r3, #100    ; 0x64
 276    10428:   603b        str r3, [r7, #0]
 277    1042a:   bf00        nop 
 278    1042c:   687b        ldr r3, [r7, #4]
 279    1042e:   1c5a        adds    r2, r3, #1
 280    10430:   607a        str r2, [r7, #4]
 281    10432:   2b09        cmp r3, #9
 282    10434:   d9fa        bls.n   1042c <main+0xc>
 283    10436:   2300        movs    r3, #0
 284    10438:   4618        mov r0, r3
```
`len(loop_finder.loops) = 1` this time.

issue_title: Discussion: signature recovery, type inference, ddg
issue_body:
I wanted to do some simple signed/unsigned type inference, based on the DDG, when I run into this problem. 

Lets start with the following simple example:
```
typedef struct st {
        int a;
        int b;
} st_t;

int f(st_t* l, st_t* r) {
        return l->a < r->a && l->b < r->b;
}
```
If I build a DDG for this (only the relevant parts are shown), I get this:
![ddg1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/759365/19252986/4689fda4-8f48-11e6-8ee4-fb14d18c2e97.png)

What we can see from it for example at `0x8048584`˛that both `l->a` and `r->a` will resolve to the same node, since the concretization of the address will be `0x0`, similarly at `0x8048592` both `l->b` and `r->b` will be resolved to the same node, with address `0x4`. This is because they are adressed as `eax+0` and `eax+4`, and `eax` will be concretized to `0` independent if we come from the `esp+4` path or the `esp+8` path.

If I prepare the state a bit before doing DDG, by setting concrete values for `l` and `r`:
```
state.stack_push(claripy.BVV(0x410000, project.arch.bits))
state.stack_push(claripy.BVV(0x400000, project.arch.bits))
state.stack_push(claripy.BVV(0x0, project.arch.bits))
```
then things get better, since `eax` will be concretized to `0x400000` and `0x410000`, but still the variables are named `Stack sp/bp+x`: 
![ddg2](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/759365/19253168/832a916e-8f49-11e6-9f84-4b51076dad8f.png)

I've added some type annotation (steal the base idea from fidget):
```
arg1 = claripy.BVV(0x410000, project.arch.bits)
arg1_struct = SimStructAbstract(label='arg1', base=0x410000)
arg1 = arg1.annotate(s_type_backend.TypeAnnotation(s_type.SimTypePointer(arg1_struct, label=[],offset=claripy.BVV(0, project.arch.bits))))
state.stack_push(arg1)
[...]
```
and patched the DDG code to check those annotations (not sure if type annotations are desinged to be used like this):
![ddg3](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/759365/19253286/510872cc-8f4a-11e6-87e6-215c4f614d6b.png)


So what I'm planning is (a bit x86 specific):
 - build the DDG, with an empty state
 - identify the input variables (bp+8, bp+12, ...) and registers
 - search for dereference of these variables
 - if found, then refine the input state, by concretizing the variables what got dereferenced
 - rebuild the DDG, with the refined state
 - search for dereference of the previously concretized variables
 - if found refine the input state, and continue this till the DDG doesnt change


What do you think about this idea? 
Any advice regarding to this topic is much appreciated!

issue_title: VehicleMode should be easy to compare for equality
issue_body:
VehicleMode should be an enum or at least enum-like. The biggest issue with the current implementation is:
```python
VehicleMode("GUIDED") == VehicleMode("GUIDED") is False
```
This means that determining which mode you are in either requires you to make your own enum:
```python
GUIDED = VehicleMode("GUIDED")
LAND = VehicleMode("LAND")
```
and only use those variables. 

Test for string equality:
```python
str(vehicle.mode) == "VehicleMode:GUIDED"`
````
Or access the `name` field (which is not documented):
```python
vehicle.mode.name == "GUIDED"
```

Additionally, being an enum would allow for code that checks whether a given mode exists programmatically:
```python
if "LAND" not in VehicleMode:
    # do something
```
and to iterate through the available enums:
```python
for mode in VehicleMode:
    menu.add(mode)
```

The type of enum I have in mind is detailed in [this StackOverflow post](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/36932/how-can-i-represent-an-enum-in-python)

issue_title: check_configuration assumes parameters to loaders are always tuples
issue_body:
Specifically:

https://github.com/django-admin-tools/django-admin-tools/blob/master/admin_tools/__init__.py#L38

    for loader in loaders:
        if isinstance(loader, (tuple, list)):
            loaders += loader[1]

This code is presumably trying to handle:

    TEMPLATE_LOADERS = (
        ('django.template.loaders.cached.Loader', (
            'django.template.loaders.filesystem.Loader',
            'django.template.loaders.app_directories.Loader',
        )),
    )

It has multiple issues:

1) It mutates settings.TEMPLATE_LOADERS - it should leave it alone.
2) It only handles 'django.template.loaders.cached.Loader', assuming that the parameter must be a tuple/list. It doesn't handle other  TEMPLATE_LOADERS which might take different parameters altogether.
3) It assumes that it TEMPLATE_LOADERS is a tuple, when it could be a list.

Better code would be something like:

    loaders = list(loaders)  # our copy
    for loader in loaders:
        if isinstance(loader, (tuple, list)):
            if len(loader) > 1:
                arg = loader[1]
                if isinstance(args, tuple):
                    # possibly CachedLoader parameters, which are themselves loaders.
                   loaders.extend(arg)

(untested)

4) It raises a hard error if the configuration is "incorrect". For my use case, I need to do something a bit different, which means that `"admin_tools.template_loaders.Loader"` is never in my `TEMPLATE_LOADERS` (we have a custom cached loader into which we can include the admin_tools loader, but not using the same parameter mechanism that the stock cached loader uses).

Instead of raising an `IncorrectlyConfigured`, this should be using the checks framework - https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.7/topics/checks/

issue_title: Update VIIRS L1B reader to support new standard file naming scheme
issue_body:
#### Problem description

The NASA official VIIRS L1B filenaming scheme has now been finalized and has been changed from what SatPy previously supported. See https://ladsweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/api/v1/productPage/product=VNP02MOD

A coworker provided me with the following mapping:

```
VNP02MOD -> L1B moderate resolution SNPP
VNP03MOD -> GEO moderate resolution SNPP
VNP02IMG -> L1B imager resolution SNPP
VNP03IMG -> GEO imager resolution SNPP 
VNP02DNB -> L1B day night band SNPP
VNP03DNB -> GEO day night band SNPP
```

Note that the ESDTs for JPSS-1 will be very similar except the NP gets switched to J1 so:
```
VJ102MOD
VJ103MOD
VJ102IMG
VJ103IMG
VJ102DNB
VJ103DNB
```

I think this should just require updating the file pattern in `viirs_l1b.yaml`.

issue_title: Missing configuration option in default .gangarc file - [defaults_DiracProxy]
issue_body:
Hello,

It looks like with the roll-out of 6.3.0 a configuration option has been missed in the default `.gangarc` file, so running via CVMFS throws an error:

```bash
ERROR    problems with loading Plugin /cvmfs/ganga.cern.ch/Ganga/install/LATEST/python/GangaDirac
```

issue_title: Double URL encoding in issuer_name param to uri_provisioning
issue_body:
#### Version: Latest (v.2.2.5)

The issuer_name parameter is first encoded [here](https://github.com/pyotp/pyotp/blob/master/src/pyotp/utils.py#L64) and the output of that is HTML encoded again [here](https://github.com/pyotp/pyotp/blob/master/src/pyotp/utils.py#L75).

![capture](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/9205510/26757534/7207432e-4884-11e7-8746-bb6f06323e68.PNG)

The first filter changes `Hello World` to `Hello%20World` (which is exactly what it should be) but the second time changes the output of the first filter (`Hello%20World`) to `Hello%2520World` (escaping the percent sign before the 20).

When authenticator apps receive this, they interpret the `%25` and render the remainder. Therefore, by using the `uri_provisioning()` method with the `issuer_name` string `"Hello World"`, the apps display `Hello%20World` instead of `Hello World`.

Pull request coming soon.

issue_title: Bug in distortion model
issue_body:
I was looking at the library's implementation of the distortion model and noticed some strange thing.
Here is the link to the source code:
https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/5e73f7bb906a3cbec8ae43e888f182cc56c18692/include/librealsense2/rsutil.h#L15

Look at how you project and deproject point into image, here are these methods
rs2_project_point_to_pixel
rs2_deproject_pixel_to_point

I have noticed that when we deproject pixel to 3d point you are applying undistort method (in the example there is Brown conrady model used).
But when we projecting 3d point back to pixel instead of distorting uv's you are undistorting them once again.

Did I missed something or maybe I don't understand something?
Lines 24-31 and 56-61 are equivalent. The common sense tells me that the code in lines 56-61 must UNDISTORT uv coordinates and lines 24-31 must DISTORT these coordinates. But they do the same thing - UNDISTORT.

issue_title: Improve performance in Travis CI builds with greater parallelization
issue_body:
Currently, when building watchmaker using Travis CI, the `check` job must be successful before the `docs` job will start which must be successful before the 6 `test` jobs can start. These 8 jobs can all be run in parallel to improve performance. 

While it is clear that performance will improve by parallelizing the jobs in the case where all 8 jobs will be successful, performance is also likely to improve in situations where 1+ job(s) fail.  Here any failure of any of the first 4-8 parallel jobs that Travis CI allocates will cause the entire build to fail. Testing more, sooner, leads to quicker discovery of errors. Further, the `check` and `docs` stages seem to rarely produce errors and thus are poorly adapted as gatekeepers but function instead as bottlenecks.

```python
import tensorflow_hub as hub

class FakeNetwork:

            batch_size=batch_size,

        )
    
    def _fake_data(self):
        text_input = np.array(

        )

        idx_input = np.array(

        emb_input = np.array([

            [5, 15, 10, 3, 15, 10, 14],
            [7, 8, 8, 12, 0, 0, 0],
            [7, 13, 12, 10, 0, 0, 0],
            [3, 8, 9, 6, 16, 0, 0]
          ]
        ])
        labels = np.random.randn(3).reshape((3, 1)).astype(np.float32)
        return {"text": text_input, "idx": idx_input, "emb": emb_input}, labels

    def _input_fn(self, features, labels, mode):
        dataset = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices((features, labels))
        dataset = dataset.repeat(self.hparams.num_epochs)
        dataset = dataset.prefetch(1024)
        dataset = dataset.batch(self.hparams.batch_size)
        iterator = dataset.make_one_shot_iterator()
        features, labels = iterator.get_next()
        return features, labels

    def _model_fn(self, features, labels, mode):
        with tf.variable_scope("chars"):
            with tf.device("/cpu:0"):
                c_embs = tf.get_variable(
                    name="c_embs",
                    dtype=tf.float32, 
                    shape=[17, 10],
                    trainable=True)

            char = tf.nn.embedding_lookup(c_embs, features['emb'])
            char_out = tf.reshape(char, [-1, 7*7*10])

        with tf.variable_scope("elmo"):
            words = tf.string_split(features['text'])
            dense_words = tf.sparse_to_dense(
                sparse_indices=words.indices,
                sparse_values=words.values,
                default_value="",
                output_shape=[self.hparams.batch_size, 7])

            elmo = hub.Module("https://tfhub.dev/google/elmo/2", trainable=False)
            elmo_emb = elmo(
                inputs={"tokens": dense_words,
                        "sequence_len": tf.cast(features['idx'], tf.int32)},
                signature="tokens",
                as_dict=True)['elmo'] 

            weights = tf.get_variable(
                name='w', dtype=tf.float32,
                shape=[7*1024, 1],
                initializer=tf.random_normal_initializer())

        elmo_rs = tf.reshape(elmo_emb, [-1, 7*1024])
        logits = tf.matmul(elmo_rs, weights)
        print(logits.get_shape().as_list())

        if mode == tf.estimator.ModeKeys.PREDICT:
            predictions = {
                "logits": logits,
            }
            export_outputs = {"prediction": tf.estimator.export.PredictOutput(predictions)}

            return tf.estimator.EstimatorSpec(
                mode=mode,
                predictions=predictions,
                export_outputs=export_outputs)

        loss = tf.square(tf.subtract(labels, logits))
        loss = tf.reduce_mean(loss)

        if mode == tf.estimator.ModeKeys.TRAIN:
            opt = tf.train.AdamOptimizer(learning_rate=1e-3)
            train_op = opt.minimize(loss=loss, global_step=tf.train.get_global_step())
            return tf.estimator.EstimatorSpec(
                mode=mode,
                loss=loss,
                train_op=train_op)


def main(argv=None):
    tf.logging.set_verbosity(tf.logging.INFO)
    fn = FakeNetwork(batch_size=3)
    features_train, labels_train = fn._fake_data()
    config = tf.estimator.RunConfig(
        model_dir="/tmp/fake",
        log_step_count_steps=100,
        save_checkpoints_steps=100,
        save_summary_steps=100)
    estimator = tf.estimator.Estimator(model_fn=fn._model_fn, config=config)
    estimator.train(input_fn=lambda: fn._input_fn(features_train, labels_train, mode=tf.estimator.ModeKeys.TRAIN))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

issue_title: Fails to show new data when event files are replaced (e.g. w/ rsync)
issue_body:
# Problem summary
We have a problem that tensorboard (1.2.1) does not show new run data when we switched to syncing the data from Google Cloud Storage to local directory.

It looks like when the tensorboard reads an event file from local directory - it will not notice that the event file was deleted and recreated (which is quite valid case when you are using gsutil rsync to sync the data from Google Cloud Storage). I have a short-term workaround (to be tested) but I think there is a need to fiix it permanently in tensorboard - it's quite a valid case to handle due to another bug - #158)  

# Some context and problem manifestation

Initially we stored data from our ML runs in Google Cloud Storage and pointed our Tensorboard to reead the the data directly from there (logdir=gs://BUCKET/FOLDER) . We run the Tensorboard in Kubernetes cluster and made it available to all our team members via oauth proxy. This is great as we can always see fresh data and any member of the team can access it there. We wanted the data to refresh quite frequently - every few minutes. This worked very well but we found that there is a cost downside to it.

Due to #158 we had a lot of GCS accesses generated by TB just scanning GCS (several millions of accesses/day). This was not only slow but also incurred relatively high GCS cost (20 USD/4 days!). 

Then we switched to another approach - we sync the data to a local folder from gcs using `gsutil -m rsync -d gs://BUCKET/FOLDER LOCAL_FOLDER`and then tensorboard points to that locally downloaded folder instead of pointing to gs:// . 

Additional information - we have the whole tree structure within that logdir directory so that we can see and compare all the different runs we've already produced. 

Unfortunately after switching to rsync we seem to have big problem. The new runs (in new subfolders) are showing up amongst the others after the runs are executed, but they never show any data for those runs until tensorboard gets restarted. It looks like tensorboard picks up some initially synced partial results from the run - but not enough to show the data -  and then it somehow blocks from reading any new data for that folder.

When I start an exact copy of Tensorboard at the same machine, it shows the data for those new runs properly - you can see the screenshot below showing the two tensorboards side-by-side. Tensorboard on the left is freshly started, tensorboard on the right has been run before the run has been started.
![tensorboards_left_good_right_stalled](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/595491/29238598-92daec54-7f39-11e7-9078-07a46b3fe285.png)

# Result of investigation
I read in detail how gsutl rsync synchronizes the data and I guessed what might be wrong. I confirmed that by looking at the `lsof` output for the long-running tensorboard instance - and it becomes quite obvious (look at the "deleted" entries):
```
tensorboa 191 root   30r   REG       8,1  1085346   8881152 /tensorboard-data/dexnet2/convnet/20171108_kcjniy6q/logs/events.out.tfevents.1502483404.sylvester (deleted)
tensorboa 191 root   31r   REG       8,1  1148462   8880988 /tensorboard-data/dexnet2/convnet/20171108_b2ere44n/logs/events.out.tfevents.1502483298.arnold (deleted)
tensorboa 191 root   32r   REG       8,1   715534   8881201 /tensorboard-data/dexnet2/convnet/20171108_silfxox7/logs/events.out.tfevents.1502402857.sylvester
tensorboa 191 root   33r   REG       8,1   930378   8880956 /tensorboard-data/dexnet2/convnet/20171108_61rgid3z/logs/events.out.tfevents.1502403170.sylvester
tensorboa 191 root   34r   REG       8,1   934806   8881119 /tensorboard-data/dexnet2/convnet/20171108_k9en6v98/logs/events.out.tfevents.1502403287.sylvester
tensorboa 191 root   35r   REG       8,1  1744410   8881036 /tensorboard-data/dexnet2/convnet/20171108_g2unt5hi/logs/events.out.tfevents.1502444237.sylvester (deleted)
tensorboa 191 root   36r   REG       8,1  5844954   8881430 /tensorboard-data/dexnet2/resnet/logs_9bli9id2/logs/events.out.tfevents.1501736695.arnold
```
The gsutil rsync works in the way that it always deletes and recreates files if they change - it never appends changes to the files. They state it in their manual, and it comes from the fact that the GCS files are in fact objects not files, and are immutable, so you don't even have a possibilty to transfer it partially - it's always all-or-nothing.

On the other hand it's quite reasonable what tensorboard does: when it has logdir set to the local folder, it opens the events* file and expects that tensorflow-based machine learning will continue appending data to it rather than delete and recreate the file when new data appears. It's completely resonable assumption - and I guess it's quite different when it accesses GCS directly (for the same reasons gsutil rsync only synchronizes full files). 

You could see in the `lsof` output that we have problem with 20171108_b2ere44n and 20171108_kcjniy6q - in both cases the file is already deleted, but tensorboard still has it open (which is quite normal behaviour in linux/ext4 - the file/inode will only be physically removed after last process keeps it open).


# Potential solutions
I see several potential solutions to the problem, and it would be great if someone from tensorboard team can confirm whether the long term solution I proposed is good.

1) Short term workaround: I will setup double syncing `gs -> gsutil rsync -> linux rsync --inplace -> logdir`. I tested that I can make the standard linux rsync works in the way that it will not delete/recreate the files but it will replace the data in place (--inplace switch) - I also considered and tested behaviour of --append and --append-verify, but I think it's safer bet to do --inplace (append-verify will silently refuse syncin for file that does not match the content prefix in the old file). The process will sync the data first to some folder with `gsutil rsync` and then sync the data to final destination with the linux's `rsync --inplace` . The obvious downsides of this workaround is that it makes our process more complex and prone to error, and we have to keep two copies of our whole log directory in our kubernetes pod. Thats's quite bad long-term when we will have a lot more data to look at.

2) Proper fix: I think tensorboard should be smarter in reading local data and check if the file has been deleted since it was last read and close/reopen/reread the file when it was. You could use fstat for that (see the second answer here https://stackoverflow.com/questions/12690281/check-if-an-open-file-has-been-deleted-after-open-in-python) Can this be done in next version please? Or maybe there is already a possible solution for current tensorboard (1.2.1) ?

issue_title: Pending Scan Logic in Mac Class Interferes with Any Active Scan
issue_body:
In [`Mac::Scan`](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/master/src/core/mac/mac.cpp#L171) if the state isn't currently `kStateIdle` then it ends up setting the `mPendingScanRequest` variable which will be used after the current task is complete.

It also sets all other `mScan*` variables which could currently be in use by a different scan. I believe all those variables need to be duplicated to save for the pending scan, and then copied to the active scan variables when it is no longer pending.

Additionally, if either of the scans are started twice while a not `kStateIdle` the second scan will overwrite the pending variable state.

When adding to the shortcut list, if one shortcut is the start of another shortcut than it will override it and pass the remainder of the longer shortcut in arg.

Example:
    Cmd.shortcuts.update({
                          'p': 'first_sc',
                          'pcon' : 'second_sc',
     })

- Your config file:
  
  ```
  [general]
  status_path = ~/.config/vdirsyncer/status
  
  
  [pair google_calendar]
  a = google_calendar_remote
  b = google_calendar_local
  collections = ["me@gmail.com"]
  conflict_resolution = "b wins"
  
  [storage google_calendar_remote]

  client_id = "notreallymyid.apps.googleusercontent.com"
  client_secret = "really_real_secret"
  token_file = /path/to/my/tokens
  
  [storage google_calendar_local]
  type = filesystem
  path = /path/to/my/calendar
  fileext = .ics
  ```
- debug output:

```
debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/cli/tasks.py", line 66, in sync_collection
debug:     force_delete=force_delete
debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/sync.py", line 228, in sync
debug:     action(a_info, b_info, conflict_resolution)
debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/sync.py", line 248, in inner
debug:     dest_href, dest_etag = dest.storage.upload(item)
debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/storage/base.py", line 17, in inner
debug:     return f(self, *args, **kwargs)
debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/storage/base.py", line 17, in inner
debug:     return f(self, *args, **kwargs)
debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/storage/base.py", line 17, in inner
debug:     return f(self, *args, **kwargs)
debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py", line 503, in upload
debug:     return self._put(href, item, None)
debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py", line 474, in _put

debug:   File "/home/wayne/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/vdirsyncer/utils/http.py", line 72, in request
debug:     raise exceptions.NotFoundError(r.reason)

```

You can probably reproduce this issue yourself. First, make sure that your Google calendar has been synced with `vdirsyncer sync`. Then, with Google calendar go create an appointment - any old appointment will do. Re-run `vdirsyncer sync` again and you should see an appointment being synced. Open this file with your favorite editor and find the line that says

```
STATUS:CONFIRMED
```

And change it to

```
STATUS:CANCELLED
```

In [section 4.8.1.11](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2445.txt) it says:

```
   Format Definition: The property is defined by the following notation:

     status     = "STATUS" statparam] ":" statvalue CRLF

     statparam  = *(";" xparam)

     statvalue  = "TENTATIVE"           ;Indicates event is
                                        ;tentative.
                / "CONFIRMED"           ;Indicates event is
                                        ;definite.
                / "CANCELLED"           ;Indicates event was
                                        ;cancelled.
        ;Status values for a "VEVENT"
```

However, when one does `vdirsyncer sync` after this it produces the above error.

I'm not 100% sure what the expected behavior here would be. For a one-time event I'm pretty sure that it should be deleted, but I'm not sure about a recurring event. For instance, we have a standing appointment every week, but this week it was cancelled. Only the one occurrence, though.

issue_title: Proxying Xpra is not working
issue_body:
I've tried to proxy [Xpra](https://www.xpra.org/) [html5 client](https://xpra.org/trac/wiki/Clients/HTML5) with `nbserverproxy`, but it doesn't seem to work.
When I start Xpra on a local machine with the
```
xpra start --bind-tcp=0.0.0.0:14500 --html=on --start=xterm
```
command and open `localhost:14500`, I see `xterm` in my browser.

I've tried to run this command from the `jupyter` terminal and then access `.../proxy/14500` URL.
First of all, the HTML page tries to load `js` (or `css`) from `.../proxy/js/` instead of `.../proxy/14500/js`. Ok, I've added a slash to the url and then the page was able to load.

But then, probably because it can't connect to websockets, the `index.html` page redirects me to `connect.html` page, which locally happens only when I stop X server. As I said, this likely means that it has problems with websockets so it thinks that the server is down.

There is Wiki page about proxying Xpra with Nginx: https://www.xpra.org/trac/wiki/Nginx
AFAIU it does something to HTTP headers so that websockets work behind the proxy.

I know about https://github.com/ryanlovett/nbnovnc/, but I would like to see Xpra working.

issue_title: PlainReporter colouring output of forbidden-import
issue_body:
When PlainReporter reports a `forbidden-import` error, it adds colorama colouring though it's not supposed to, resulting in colouring tokens littering plain text files. 
E.g.: running `check_all(reporter=PlainReporter)` on the following code:
```python
import sys
pass
```
results in the following plain-text output (snippet):
```
. . .
    [Line 1] You may not import any modules - you imported \x1b[34msys\x1b[0m on line 1.
. . .
```
(Note the extraneous `\x1b[34m` and `\x1b[0m`.)
The problem seems to be irrelevant of the module being imported.

issue_title: Increase readability for complex objective functions
issue_body:
Hi everyone,

in my application the decision variables undergo numerous operations inside the objective function.
If I implement the objective function in a single line of code, it would probably be 1000 characters long and be totally unreadable.
How can I make this code more readable without adding additional overhead to the model?

Currently, I have introduced intermediate decision variables (model.addVar()) for readability.
To eliminate these intermediate variables to the solver, I have added additional equality constraints (model.addCons()).
These additional constraints make the intermediate decision variables completely dependent on the actual decision variables.
However, this process makes the model more complicated and potentially slower.

Is there any existing solution to this or is someone thinking about implementing it?

issue_title: [xray] Using gflags or similar for argument parsing
issue_body:
Did a quick search through the issues list and didn't see anything related. Since the raylet code is fast-moving, it may be helpful to rely on an argument parsing library for ```raylet``` and ```raylet_monitor``` rather than magic numbers for positional arguments.

Should be a small change, but raising as an issue rather than PR first because there may be some reason that the team doesn't want to do this.

issue_title: Seemingly poor scheduling for batch of GPU jobs
issue_body: I'm running a batch algorithm, where I have 144 tasks per batch, each requiring 1 GPU, and I have 160 GPUs in the ray cluster (40 machines x 4 gpus each). However, Ray seems to make odd scheduling decisions that sometimes require one GPU to do two jobs in a given batch. See figure. Note that in the first batch, each GPU does one job, but in the second batch, some GPUs are left idle and others do two jobs. I tried constructing a minimum example but was unable to reproduce at a smaller scale. Any idea what's happening or how to fix it?

issue_title: Invalid "from" formed when queue email is not specified and default has a specific form.
issue_body:
Queue's _from_address method will return an invalid value if both of the following are true:
- The queue does not have an email address specified
- DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL (in settings.py) is of the form `Mr Foo <foo@example.com>`

To be precise, it will return `NO QUEUE EMAIL ADDRESS DEFINED <Mr Foo <foo@example.com>>`

issue_title: Ingest and stacker should enable compression on stored metadata documents
How to start up a complex task tree in an orderly way, given spawn's non-determinism?
issue_body:
[Text below was posted by @matham as a comment on #32; I'm splitting it off into a new issue to keep the threads tidy]

So I kinda just ran into this. I have a parent and a bunch of children (ordered).

Naively I tried:

```py
def init_start(self):
    init_things  # get things started

async def wait_to_finish_work(self):
    await do_things

async def start(self):
    self.init_start()
    async with trio.open_nursery() as nursery:
        for child in self.children:
            nursery.spawn(self.start)
        nursery.spawn(self.wait_to_finish_work)

>>> await root.start()
```

I was hoping that the children would spawn deterministically in order and at least run until the first await in `wait_to_finish_work`. This would start the tree in **depth first order**. However, as I noticed in the result and in the design above, the initial spawning is also not in order.

Specifically, you mention that newly spawned tasks may inherit their parent's `vtime`. I wonder if it may not be better that the nursery initially *birth* its children and start their life until the first suspension in the deterministic order. Spawn *looks* deterministic and the docs don't really say much either way.

The alternative for a case like mine where you want to start off each spawnee in order in current trio would be to re-write my example as:

```py
def init_start(self):
    init_things  # get things started

async def wait_to_finish_work(self):
    await do_things

async def start(self):
    for child in self.children:
        child.init_start()
    async with trio.open_nursery() as nursery:
        for child in self.children:
            nursery.spawn(self.start)
        nursery.spawn(self.wait_to_finish_work)

>>> root.init_start()
>>> await root.start()
```
The second version is not that different, but it does make it more complex, especially for cleanup as the parent starts it in two methods. But also, this is now **breadth first order**, where at each level a node  is picked randomly to start off its children.

Edit: I'm thinking that maybe my model of spawn is wrong. E.g.

```py
async with trio.open_nursery() as nursery:
    nursery.spawn(a)
    nursery.spawn(b)
    nursery.spawn(c)
```

Looks to me like 

```py
async with trio.open_nursery() as nursery:
    magic await a()
    magic await b()
    magic await c()
```
Where the magic collects the tasks at the `with` exit and runs them side by side. Perhaps I'm wrong and the better analogy is:

```py
t1 = Thread(target=a)
t2 = Thread(target=b)
t3 = Thread(target=c)
for t in (t1, t2, t3):
    t.start()
for t in (t1, t2, t3):
    t.join()
```
because with threads order is completely undefined.

issue_title: How do we work with ingestion end points?
issue_body:
We currently assume that the data lands in an S3 bucket and we are blind to the ingestion endpoint that is upstream. 

We need to understand our users' setup and build connectors/integrate with existing ingestion mechanisms like Kafka. 

The purpose is to reduce the barriers to adoption.

issue_title: [Feature Request] - Verbose option for the Layer API
issue_body:
Hello dear friends,

I have used TL for quite a while and I'm really thankful for all the amazing work.

One thing which appeared very cool to me from the beginning was the verbose graph definition which helped me a lot during debug and development. However, I figured out a bit later that are no way to deactivate the followings:

```shell
  [TL] InputLayer  encoder/input: (?, 256, 256, 1)
  [TL] Conv2dLayer encoder/h1/conv2d: shape:[5, 5, 1, 64] strides:[1, 2, 2, 1] pad:VALID act:identity
  [TL] BatchNormLayer encoder/h1/batch_norm: decay:0.900000 epsilon:0.000010 act:identity is_train:True
```

It is a really interesting feature to have these information, however it could rapidly clutter the console output. 

#### Feature Request

The idea would be to make this output optional (default = True or False). I think there could be different ways to do this.

##### 1. Solution - Create a **verbose** parameter in the Layer API
Simple and backward compatible, a "verbose" parameter can be added to the Layer Class and influence the behavior of [print_params()](https://github.com/tensorlayer/tensorlayer/blob/master/tensorlayer/layers.py#L321) method.

##### 2. Use the Logging module from TF.

Why should we re-invent the wheel ? Everything is already implemented in Tensorflow.
We can use the logging level already existing in TF.

```python
tf.logging._level_names   ## outputs => {50: 'FATAL', 40: 'ERROR', 30: 'WARN', 20: 'INFO', 10: 'DEBUG'}
tf.logging.get_verbosity() ## outputs => 30

tf.logging.set_verbosity(tf.logging.DEBUG)
tf.logging.get_verbosity() ## outputs => 10
```

We could for instance determine that for logging level <= 20 (INFO & DEBUG), we normally output the Tensorlayer informations, and we don't perform this action for any higher value.


All the best,

Jonathan

issue_title: DateTime literals with offsets fail to convert to Python
issue_body:
vfaronov, 2010-02-10T09:46:54.000Z 
Literal.toPython() returns self if the literal is an xsd:dateTime with a
UTC offset specified, like this: 2010-02-10T12:36:00+03:00.

Handling timezones isn't straightforward, but there's a short (~100 lines)
module called iso8601[1] that can convert such values (more or less)
properly, including timezone info. It's MIT licensed. Perhaps it could be
included in the distribution, and _strToDateTime() made to use it?

[1] http://code.google.com/p/pyiso8601/source/browse/trunk/iso8601/iso8601.py

issue_title: Constructing a literal from another literal does not copy language or datatype tag
issue_body:
gromgull, 2012-01-15T18:20:03.000Z 
In [15]: b=rdflib.Literal("2", datatype=rdflib.XSD.integer)

In [16]: b
Out[16]: rdflib.term.Literal(u'2', datatype=rdflib.term.URIRef('http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer'))

In [17]: rdflib.Literal(b)==b
Out[17]: False

In [18]: rdflib.Literal(b)
Out[18]: rdflib.term.Literal(u'2')

----

In [9]: a=rdflib.Literal("cake",lang="en")

In [10]: a
Out[10]: rdflib.term.Literal(u'cake', lang='en')

In [11]: rdflib.Literal(a)==a
Out[11]: False

In [14]: rdflib.Literal(a)
Out[14]: rdflib.term.Literal(u'cake')

The constructor thinks it's just a string - we should probably check for literals and copy it completely.
